per_event_results.nary_reason: Multiple instigators (Walder Frey, Lame Lothar, Roose Bolton co-plotted; Tywin countenanced), multiple direct executors (Roose Bolton, Ryman Frey, Hosteen Frey, Raymund Frey, Black Walder, Frey crossbowmen, Bolton men-at-arms), multiple victims (Robb Stark, Catelyn Stark, Dacey Mormont, and many northern lords), and this is the canonical named set-piece massacre that dozens of other edges reference.

Instigator (Petyr Baelish) is distinct from physical executor (Lady Olenna); multiple agents in the execution chain (Olenna extracted the poison stone, Dontos delivered the hair net, Sansa unwittingly carried it); plus this is a named set-piece referenced throughout the series.

Instigator (Stannis, whose life force powered the shadow) is distinct from executor (Melisandre, who performed the shadowbinding ritual) and victim (Renly); three separate roles across three participants.
Pre-declared clean dyad: Jaime Lannister kills Aerys II Targaryen. Single agent + single victim, no instigator or ordering third party. Per D8 gate: do not reify.

Pre-declared clean dyad: Tyrion Lannister kills Tywin Lannister with a crossbow. Single agent + single victim, acting alone. Per D8 gate: do not reify. (Also: Tyrion strangles Shae is a separate clean dyad, same rule.)

Multiple distinct agents (Petyr Baelish physically, House Lannister/gold cloaks as executors), multiple commanders (Cersei and Joffrey), instigator≠executor throughout, and multiple victims (Ned captured, his sworn men and Syrio killed); this is also a named set-piece referenced repeatedly across the narrative.
Instigator (Joffrey) is distinct from executor (Ilyn Payne); involves a named weapon (Ice) and a named location, making this a reifiable n-ary event rather than a clean dyad.

Multiple victims (Pycelle and Kevan), multiple agents (Varys plus his child little-birds who killed Pycelle), and an instigating beneficiary (Aegon) distinct from the executor — this is a named set-piece with at least three distinct causal roles.

Multiple distinct executors (Drogon burns Kraznys; Grey Worm leads Unsullied assault), instigator (Daenerys) did not personally kill, multiple victims (Kraznys mo Nakloz and the Good Masters collectively), and it is a named set-piece referenced across the narrative arc.

Multiple commanders with distinct roles (Dany as instigator who stayed in pavilion vs. Jorah/Grey Worm/Daario who executed in the field), multiple distinct forces (Unsullied on flanks, Dothraki wedge through center, Stormcrows at rear), a named set-piece battle referenced by other graph edges, and both attacking agents and a defeated defending commander.

Robb Stark is both the sole orderer and sole executor of the sentence — instigator equals executor, single victim, no separate commanding third party. This is a structurally clean dyad. The prior n-ary event (Karstark's murder of the Lannister hostages) is a distinct event node.

Named set-piece battle with multiple commanders on both sides (Tyrion, Tywin, Stannis, Davos, Loras, Mace Tyrell), instigators distinct from executors (Stannis commands from the rear while Davos leads the fleet), and a contested city as the location.
